title: persona update --field without other flags bails before fields are processed
description:
In cli.rs lines 147-171, `persona::update()` is called before the `--field` processing block. When only `--field` is passed (no `--display-name`, `--bio`, `--avatar`, or `--header`), `persona::update()` bails at persona.rs line 87-88 with 'nothing to update' before the fields code on cli.rs line 156 ever runs.

What goes wrong: `broadside persona update myuser --field "Website=https://example.com"` errors out with 'nothing to update' instead of setting the metadata field. Users must always pass a dummy `--display-name` (or similar) alongside `--field`, which is unintuitive.

Fix: either move the `--field` processing into `persona::update()`, or in cli.rs check `fields.is_empty()` before calling `persona::update()` and skip it when only fields are provided.

Locations:
- /home/mark/PROJECT/broadside/src/cli.rs lines 147-171
- /home/mark/PROJECT/broadside/src/persona.rs lines 87-88
close_reason: CLI now checks has_profile_update before calling persona::update(), allowing --field-only invocations
